Version History Spike — Ireland (IE)
=====================================

Date: 2026-04-13
Test law: Environment (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 2015 (No. 29/2015)

Source 1: Irish Statute Book (irishstatutebook.ie)
--------------------------------------------------
- URL: https://www.irishstatutebook.ie/eli/2015/act/29/enacted/en/xml
- Result: Returns XML of the act AS ENACTED (original text from 2015)
- Only one version available: the enacted text
- No archive URLs, no version parameter, no point-in-time endpoint
- CONCLUSION: ISB provides ONLY "as enacted" text. No historical versions.

Source 2: Revised Acts (revisedacts.lawreform.ie)
-------------------------------------------------
- URL: https://revisedacts.lawreform.ie/eli/2015/act/29/revised/en/html
- Result: Returns consolidated HTML "Updated to 31 July 2024"
- Amendment annotations present: F1-F9+ with dates and source SIs
- Example annotation: "F1 Repealed (31.07.2024) by European Union (...) Regulations 2024 (S.I. No. 356 of 2024)"
- NO version selector, NO previous versions navigation, NO point-in-time URL parameter
- Only ~560 of ~4,041 acts are available on Revised Acts (14%)
- CONCLUSION: Provides current consolidated text only, not intermediate versions.

Source 3: Oireachtas API (api.oireachtas.ie)
--------------------------------------------
- URL: https://api.oireachtas.ie/v1/legislation?act_year=2015&act_no=29
- Result: Returns metadata + link to PDF, no text content
- versions array: [{"showAs": "As enacted", "lang": "eng", ...}] — only one version
- CONCLUSION: Catalog/metadata only, no text, no version history.

GATE RESULT: FAIL
=================
Cannot extract 2 distinct text versions with dates from any single source.

- ISB: only enacted text (1 version)
- Revised Acts: only current consolidated text (1 version), no intermediate versions
- Oireachtas API: no text content at all

DECISION: Proceed with SINGLE-SNAPSHOT ship (as enacted text from ISB XML).

Justification:
1. Ireland's 4,041 acts with clean XML are a valuable corpus even as enacted text
2. The "as enacted" text IS the official published version — it has legal value
3. Revised Acts could be added later as a Phase 2 to provide "current consolidated"
   for the ~560 acts they cover (but still only 2 versions: enacted + current)
4. Full point-in-time history may become available if Ireland implements the EU
   open data directive more fully (as other EU countries have)
5. The amendment annotations in Revised Acts (F1, F2, ...) record WHEN and HOW
   each section changed — these could theoretically be used to reconstruct
   intermediate versions, but this would require diffing and is unreliable

Follow-up tasks:
- Phase 2: Add Revised Acts HTML scraping for ~560 consolidated acts
- Phase 3: Monitor if ISB or LRC adds point-in-time API
